summary: R2 systems re-review ACCEPT. The requested R3 fixes are present and independently verified: bulk appointment validation now rejects collective final primary-office conflicts with stable bulk-command-rejected per-item reason strings before mutation, enfeoffment validates grantedByPolityId against state.m3.polities before authority/mutation, and polity actor authority is now derived from an existing M3 polity row rather than string equality alone. The inspected changes remain inside sim-core/tests/project-message scope, preserve player/AI GameCommand symmetry, keep state.m3 hash/save refusal behavior intact, and do not add client authority, hidden AI planner behavior, or new dependencies.

tests_run.result: Observed modified protocol/sim-core/test files plus project coordination changes and outbox/route files. R2 review did not modify product, test, task, or docs files.

tests_run.command: node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e M3 bulk collective primary-conflict repro
tests_run.result: Previous repro now rejects as { status: rejected, code: bulk-command-rejected, path: payload.items, message: 'sim.appoint-offices-bulk rejected items: a=office-primary-conflict;b=office-primary-conflict' }; runtime hash unchanged.

tests_run.command: node --experimental-strip-types --input-type=module -e M3 enfeoff unknown grantor authority repro
tests_run.result: Previous repro now rejects as { status: rejected, code: bad-id, path: payload.grantedByPolityId, message: 'sim.enfeoff-district references a missing granting PolityId.' }; runtime hash unchanged.
No forbidden random, real-time, DOM/window, storage, or timer API matches in sim-core/protocol source.
No React, Pixi, Electron, Node fs/path import matches in sim-core/protocol source.
11 test files passed; 65 tests passed. Includes R2 regression coverage for collective bulk primary-office conflict and unknown enfeoff granting polity.
Node/worker hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic canaries matched; root Vitest 21 files/123 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001
rg -n combined boundary scan with mixed quotes
tests_run.result: Reviewer command composition error in PowerShell: unterminated string before useful scan. Replaced by the two simpler rg scans above, both returning no matches.

decisions: The R1 REQUEST_CHANGES items are resolved: no reproduced path falls through to invariant-violation for the targeted bulk collective conflict or unknown grantor polity case.
Bulk appointment remains atomic and now validates final primary-office holder occupancy before applyM3AppointmentItems/commitRuntimeState.
Per-item rejection ordering remains stable by itemId and uses domain reason strings, including office-primary-conflict for collective conflicts.
Enfeoffment now checks grantedByPolityId exists in state.m3.polities before authority and mutation.
actorHasPolityAuthority now requires a matching state.m3 polity row for polity:<id> authority; office:<id> authority remains state-derived from existing held office state.
No save-format migration was introduced; requestSaveV1 continues intentionally refusing state.m3 before emitting incompatible save bytes.
risks: Bulk appointment semantics are intentionally conservative for M3: final collective conflicts reject the implicated batch items, but richer simultaneous role-swap semantics are not introduced in this milestone.
M3 save/load remains intentionally unsupported until the scheduled M3 save-runtime task; this review only confirms the existing refusal remains intact.
QA should still perform independent acceptance on the expanded test matrix and user-facing protocol expectations.
requested_action: Route to qa_reviewer for independent QA review, or continue the lead-controlled acceptance flow for M3-APPOINTMENT-ENFEOFFMENT-001.
